mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals expanding labor income and aggregate demand, which translates into positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month changes in PAYEMS show a rank correlation below 0.03 with 20-day forward SPX returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, restrictive monetary policy elevates discount rates and debt-service costs, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods where FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, the average 60-day forward SPX return is positive and statistically indistinguishable from zero (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in real retail sales (RSAFS) and industrial production (INDPRO) jointly signal a contraction in both final demand and supply-chain activity, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The interaction effect is stronger than the sum of individual series effects.
failure_condition: The joint decline condition does not produce negative 20-day forward returns in > 55% of observations, or the interaction term's coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) in a regression over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days when the federal funds rate exceeds 4%, because inflation shocks compress real margins and force tighter monetary policy.
failure_condition: During periods with FEDFUNDS > 4%, month-over-month CPIAUCSL increases are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in more than 50% of instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month contraction in industrial production (INDPRO) signals weakening corporate output and profit margins, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month changes in INDPRO show a positive rank correlation with 60-day forward SPX returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A three-month decline in housing starts (HOUST) exceeding 10% signals a collapse in construction activity and household wealth effects, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Instances of 3-month HOUST declines exceeding 10% are not followed by negative 5-day SPX returns in > 55% of cases over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP (GDPC1) generates positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days because it reflects aggregate income and spending momentum not yet priced by equities.
failure_condition: Quarter-over-quarter changes in GDPC1 exhibit a negative rank correlation with 20-day forward SPX returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate (UNRATE) above 5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions and falling consumer spending power, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Periods with UNRATE > 5% show average 60-day forward SPX returns that are positive and not significantly different from the full-sample mean (t-test p > 0.05).
